suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify which companion-skill, architecture-read, and RED-first-test steps are truly mandatory for small fix/validation sessions, or require workers to record deviations explicitly when they skip those steps.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The new fix reviews for `fix-editor-hardcoded-css-tokens` and `fix-hotkey-recorder-shifted-keys` both found the same mismatch between the `frontend-redesign` skill and actual accepted practice: workers skipped documented steps like invoking `frontend-design` at session start and/or running an explicit RED-first phase, yet handoffs still reported `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` with no deviations. Similar evidence already appeared in round 1 across multiple editor feature reviews.
